mechanism_narrative: Higher real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS level and subsequent 60-day SPX returns fails to exceed 0.20, or t-statistic of predictive regression is below 1.96 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, tight monetary policy increases the cost of capital and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following FEDFUNDS > 5% triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit ratio of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%) signal an overheating economy likely to prompt aggressive Fed tightening, which depresses equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The decile spread between high and low regimes of this interaction yields a Rank IC near zero (absolute < 0.02) over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During economic contractions (NBER-defined recession periods), an increase in industrial production signals the beginning of recovery and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following positive INDPRO growth in recessions is not statistically higher than in expansions (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth annualized exceeds 3%, the economy is expanding at an above-trend pace, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and supports equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following GDP > 3% triggers is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05), or the hit ratio is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200,000 indicate a robust labor market, reinforcing consumer confidence and spending, which positively impacts equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following strong payrolls reports is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) or the directional accuracy is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (headline CPI YoY below 2%), a rise in inflation toward the Fed's target signals healthy demand and reflation, leading to positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The forward returns in the low-inflation regime are not positively associated with CPI changes (regression coefficient not significant at 5% level).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million annualized, it signals strong housing demand and overall economic vitality, which boosts equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following HOUST > 1500 triggers is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) or the precision of positive days is below 60%.
